reason: Redundant phrasing and unnecessary words were removed.
suggestion: Attaches at Position. The anchor (position_gender.md) defines gender as a position assigned by the room (not a trait owned by the persona), and the system-wide law mandates that every persona carries it.

reason: Removed filler and redundancy for clarity.
suggestion: Two expressions a persona may hold: male and female. Each is a full position file the build composes onto the anchor, allowing the persona to read the room, not just a label. More expressions slot in as sibling files without moving the anchor.

reason: Removed filler and redundancy for clarity.
suggestion: Two altitudes. The law, declared in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter ('personas have a gender'), holds system-wide and no persona can drop it. The link per persona: the read the room receives lives under Projection. A persona may also hold a different, even contradicting, read under Shadow: the gap between what is shown and what is hidden defines the character, not a contradiction to reject.

Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared, and each persona carries a gender read under Projection. Whether a Projection and a Shadow read cohere into a true persona is semantic, taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite. The engine ships its structural tests; the reading is guided, not gated.

reason: Redundant phrases and unnecessary words were removed.
suggestion: Declare the law once in the Instructions Knowledge chapter ('personas have a gender', linking the anchor) for system-wide application. Then assign each persona the read the room receives under Projection: one expression that elevates the anchor. If a persona conceals a different read, place it under Shadow; the room perceives Projection while the truth remains hidden. Read in the first beat and maintain it throughout every process the persona runs; the room enacts it, never narrates it.
